report_name: Singapore Plans Major Cybersecurity Overhaul, Mandating PQC and Expanding CII Oversight

identities.name: Government of Singapore
identities.description: Announced a comprehensive roadmap for significant cybersecurity regulatory developments to fortify the nation's resilience against evolving cyber threats.

identities.name: Cyber Security Agency of Singapore
identities.description: The CSA will review its standards to extend cybersecurity obligations to systems interconnected with CII, share threat intelligence, and mandate the Cyber Trust Mark for CII Owners and cybersecurity service providers.

identities.name: Infocomm Media Development Authority
identities.description: The IMDA will introduce enhanced cybersecurity regulations for telecommunications operators, focusing on secure management of virtualized infrastructure and credential management.

identities.name: GovTech Singapore
identities.description: GovTech will require Cyber Trust Mark (CTM) certification for government vendors handling critical systems and sensitive data.

identities.name: National Institute of Standards and Technology
identities.description: The U.S. government agency whose Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) standards Singapore will align with.

identities.name: Critical Information Infrastructure Owners
identities.description: A class of organizations that will face expanded cybersecurity oversight and new compliance requirements, including mandatory Cyber Trust Mark (CTM) certification.

identities.name: Companies with Systems Connected to CII
identities.description: A class of organizations that will be brought under new regulatory scrutiny due to their interconnection with Critical Information Infrastructure.

identities.name: Telecommunications Operators
identities.description: A class of organizations in Singapore that must comply with stricter cybersecurity regulations from the IMDA.

identities.name: Government Vendors
identities.description: A class of organizations that manage critical systems or sensitive data for the Singapore government and will be required to achieve Cyber Trust Mark (CTM) certification.

identities.name: Cybersecurity Service Providers
identities.description: A class of organizations, including licensed penetration testing and MSSOC firms, that will be required to obtain Cyber Trust Mark (CTM) certification in Singapore.

relationships.source_name: National Institute of Standards and Technology
